title: Airbnb property management on the French Riviera: how to monetize your apartment in 2026
description: You own an apartment or house on the French Riviera but do not live there all year round? Short-term rental can turn that property into a significant income str
bodyHtml:
You own an apartment or house on the French Riviera but do not live there all year round? Short-term rental can turn that property into a significant income stream. Provided you choose the right management approach.

Why the French Riviera is perfect for short-term rentals

The Riviera benefits from one of Europe's longest tourist seasons. High season runs from May to late October, with peaks in July-August and during events (Cannes Film Festival, Monaco Grand Prix, Nice Jazz Festival). This extended season allows well-managed properties to achieve annual occupancy rates of 65 to 75%.

A well-located 2-bedroom apartment in Nice or Antibes can generate between €18,000 and €28,000 in gross annual revenue. A prestige villa with pool in Cannes can exceed €80,000 in high season alone.

Nice regulations 2025-2026

Nice tightened its short-term rental regulations in 2025. Any property listed on Airbnb, Booking or Vrbo must now be registered with the town hall (obtaining a registration number mandatory on all listings). In some high-density zones, annual rental day caps may apply. Property owners who had already registered before the rules came into force retain their valid permits — this is the case for all properties managed by YourBnB06.

What does a property management service cost on the Riviera?

Standard market commission runs between 18 and 25% of gross rental income. YourBnB06 charges a fixed rate of 20% VAT included, with no hidden fees. Cleaning between stays is charged directly to guests (standard Airbnb practice), preserving the owner's net yield.

The YourBnB06 × Élodie Fleury advantage

Entrusting your property to YourBnB06 means benefiting from dual expertise: a manager who knows premium short-term rental codes AND an estate agent who can value, list or sell your property if your plans change. The only service on the Riviera combining both dimensions under one contact.

Want to maximise your Riviera property income?

Élodie provides a free rental income simulation for your property. Reply within 48 hours.

faqs.question: How much can you earn renting an apartment on Airbnb in Nice?
faqs.answer: A well-located 2-bedroom apartment in Nice (Old Town, Promenade, City Centre) can generate between €18,000 and €28,000 in gross annual revenue at 65 to 75% occupancy. A centrally located studio typically earns €12,000 to €18,000 per year. These figures vary significantly with location, presentation, photo quality and dynamic pricing. A professional manager can increase revenue by 15 to 30% compared to unoptimised self-management.

faqs.question: Do you need a permit to rent on Airbnb in Nice?
faqs.answer: Yes, since 2025. Nice has implemented a mandatory registration system for all short-term tourist rentals. Owners must register with the town hall and obtain a registration number to be displayed on all listings. In some zones, annual day caps may apply. Non-registration can result in fines of up to €5,000 per undeclared rental. A professional concierge service handles these administrative steps on behalf of the owner.

faqs.question: What is YourBnB06 and how does the concierge service work?
faqs.answer: YourBnB06 is a premium short-term rental concierge service founded by Élodie Fleury, active on the French Riviera from Cannes to Menton. The service handles everything: listing creation and optimisation on Airbnb, Booking and Vrbo, reservation management, guest welcome, professional cleaning and incident management. Commission is fixed at 20% VAT included of gross rental income. Contact: info@elodiefleury.fr or WhatsApp +33 6 27 12 14 13.

faqs.question: Can you entrust your property to YourBnB06 from abroad (Spain, Latin America)?
faqs.answer: Yes — that is precisely the core value of YourBnB06. Many owners are Spanish or Latin American residents who use their property only a few weeks per year. Full remote management is provided: key handover to guests, inventory, cleaning, payments. Élodie communicates in Spanish, French and English, eliminating any language barrier. Monthly reports and payments are sent regularly.

faqs.question: How long is the tourist season on the French Riviera?
faqs.answer: The French Riviera has one of Europe's longest tourist seasons. High season runs from May to late October (6 months), with peaks in July-August and during major events (Cannes Film Festival in May, Monaco Grand Prix, Nice Jazz Festival). The off-season is not dead: Christmas, winter and spring school holidays also generate significant occupancy. A well-managed property can achieve annual occupancy rates of 65 to 75%, versus 40 to 50% for non-professional management.
